heroHeadline: CJNG Crisis Day 58 — Teotihuacan Shooting Kills Canadian Tourist, US Embassy Staff Killed in Chihuahua, USMCA Round Scheduled
heroSubtitle: A gunman killed a Canadian tourist and injured 13 others at the Teotihuacan pyramids on April 20 — 51 days before the FIFA World Cup opening. Two US Embassy instructor officers and two Mexican AEI officials were killed in a convoy crash in Chihuahua (Apr 19) after a joint drug lab raid, triggering a diplomatic row after Sheinbaum said she was not informed of the operation. USTR Greer and Economy Secretary Ebrard agreed April 20 to hold the first formal USMCA bilateral negotiating round the week of May 25 in Mexico City. Sheinbaum promises investigations into both incidents at April 21 mañanera.
footerNote: Aggregated intelligence from all Mexico-based Watchboard trackers. Data sourced from SEDENA, SESNSP, InSight Crime, OHCHR, and major international outlets.
